response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer with a strong web development background and some low-latency systems work, but the role requires deep hardware expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design. There is no evidence of GPU architecture experience, deep-learning workload optimization, SIMD/MIMD architecture work, or any silicon-level performance engineering. Despite 15+ years of experience, the domain mismatch is fundamental for a principal-level hardware performance architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/second)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, open-source authorship, C/C++ systems work at National Instruments
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, system architecture design, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is centered on full-stack web/product development, not principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU optimization expertise
Long tenure with some low-latency systems and C/C++ exposure
